extraction.one_line_summary: HIRATA Intermarché won from reduced breakaway sprint, outfoxing WRIGHT Fred who was strongest but led out and faded in final meters.
tactical_patterns: WRIGHT Fred strongest in break but led out from too far, demonstrating inexperience costs wins despite superior power.
Trek drove tempo on Cat-1 climb to eliminate non-climbing sprinters, targeting BENNETT Sam's green jersey specifically.
EVENEPOEL Remco first GC leader to hold jersey consecutive days reveals other teams conceded mid-mountain stage control.

rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: positive: Clearly strongest in breakaway but tactically naive, leading out sprint from front and fading—raw power present.
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Trek executing green jersey takeover strategy on selective stages where BENNETT Sam cannot survive climbing.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: First repeat red jersey wearer signals peloton acknowledging his GC strength after Stage 6 mountain dominance.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Lost major time to EVENEPOEL on Stage 6, now sitting well back in GC standings.
